ogTitle: Gammon Ranges vs Gawler: Climate Safety

heading: Gammon Ranges vs Gawler — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gammon Ranges, South Australia and Gawler, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gawler has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Gammon Ranges (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gammon Ranges or Gawler safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gammon Ranges has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Gawler scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gammon Ranges scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Gawler scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gammon Ranges or Gawler?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gawler has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
